answer:
\boxed{A}

The protrusion of gingiva beyond rubber dam margins is most commonly due to **edematous (swollen) gingival tissues**, which can occur from trauma, inflammation, or excessive pressure during placement. Holes placed too far apart (B) or too close together (D) would typically cause gaps or excessive tension, not gingival protrusion. A lightweight dam (C) is less likely to cause this issue unless it’s improperly secured.
